question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted StreamFile whose fee-bearing RecordItems have been mutated after hash verification but before verified() dispatches to the parser, causing the mirror node to persist incorrect transaction fees that diverge from the on-chain consensus record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verified() accepts a raw StreamFile<?> with no post-verification fee field re-validation, allowing a race-condition window between Downloader.onVerified() hash check and BatchStreamFileNotifier queue insertion to swap fee amounts in the StreamFile object?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the CompositeStreamFileNotifier to call verified() on a StreamFile containing zero-fee transactions by injecting a crafted stream file into the cloud storage bucket that passes signature threshold but carries manipulated charged_tx_fee values, causing the mirror node to report fees as zero?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause BatchStreamFileNotifier.verified() to route a RECORD-type StreamFile with inflated max_fee fields through the recordStreamFileSubscriber queue, resulting in the mirror node's REST API reporting transaction fees higher than those actually charged on-chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of fee-range validation inside verified() to pass a StreamFile whose RecordItems contain negative charged_tx_fee values, causing the mirror node database to store negative fees and corrupt downstream fee analytics?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFile with duplicate transaction records carrying different fee amounts and pass it through verified(), exploiting the lack of deduplication in the notifier interface to cause the parser to persist the higher-fee duplicate and overwrite the correct fee?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CompositeStreamFileNotifier's sequential iteration over notifiers so that if the first notifier throws an exception after partially persisting fee data, subsequent notifiers are skipped, leaving the fee state in an inconsistent intermediate value?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the BatchStreamFileNotifier's ArrayBlockingQueue to reach capacity by flooding the system with valid stream files, causing queue.put() to block indefinitely and preventing legitimate fee-bearing record files from being processed, effectively suppressing fee records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verified() does not validate that the StreamFile's consensusEnd timestamp is monotonically increasing, allowing a replayed older StreamFile with different fee data to be re-ingested and overwrite current fee records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFile with a valid hash chain but with assessed_custom_fee entries set to arbitrarily large values into the verified() pipeline, causing the mirror node to report inflated custom fees to API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamFileNotifier interface's lack of authentication on the StreamFile parameter to pass a StreamFile whose getType() returns RECORD but whose items contain BALANCE-type fee structures, causing the BatchStreamFileNotifier to route fee data to the wrong parser and corrupt fee records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mutable nature of the StreamFile object passed to verified() to modify fee fields between the time BatchStreamFileNotifier places it in the buffer and the time StreamFileSubscriber.handle() flushes it to the parser, resulting in incorrect fee persistence?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the BatchStreamFileNotifier's flush-interval logic by timing stream file submissions to force a premature batch flush that includes a fee-manipulated StreamFile before the full consensus set is assembled, causing partial or incorrect fee data to be persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verified() in BatchStreamFileNotifier throws IllegalArgumentException for unsupported StreamFile types, causing an unhandled exception that disrupts the executor thread and silently drops subsequent fee-bearing record files from the queue?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFile with a valid signature but with the charged_tx_fee field set to the maximum long value, pass it through verified(), and cause arithmetic overflow in downstream fee aggregation logic, resulting in incorrect total fee calculations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CompositeStreamFileNotifier's lack of transactional semantics across its notifier list so that a fee-bearing StreamFile is partially processed by some notifiers but not others, leaving the system in a state where fee data is inconsistent across different storage backends?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamFileNotifier.verified() interface's acceptance of a wildcard generic StreamFile<?> to pass a StreamFile implementation that overrides getItems() to return fee-manipulated items lazily, causing different fee values to be seen by different consumers of the same StreamFile?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a fee-bounds check in verified() to submit a StreamFile where fractional custom fees have a denominator of zero, causing a division-by-zero error in the fee calculation pipeline and potentially skipping fee persistence for the affected transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchStreamFileNotifier's unsynchronized buffer (ArrayList) by triggering concurrent verified() calls from multiple downloader threads, causing a race condition that corrupts the fee-bearing StreamFile buffer and results in incorrect batch fee processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CutoverServiceImpl.verified() implementation to inject a RecordFile with manipulated fee schedule data during the block-stream-to-record-stream cutover window, causing the lastRecordFile reference to be updated with incorrect fee baseline data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verified() does not validate the StreamFile's node field against a trusted allowlist, allowing a StreamFile sourced from an untrusted node to pass through the notifier and persist manipulated transaction fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockStreamVerifier calling streamFileNotifier.verified(recordFile) after blockFileTransformer.transform() to inject fee manipulation during the block-to-record transformation step, where the transformed RecordFile's fee fields are not re-verified against the original block hash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamFileNotifier interface's lack of idempotency guarantees to cause the same fee-bearing StreamFile to be passed to verified() twice (e.g., via a retry mechanism), resulting in duplicate fee records being persisted in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchStreamFileNotifier's maxItems flush threshold by crafting a StreamFile with an artificially inflated item count (getCount()), causing the batch to flush prematurely and process fee data out of the intended ordering?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of fee schedule version validation in verified() to pass a StreamFile referencing an outdated fee schedule, causing the mirror node to apply stale fee rates when computing and storing assessed custom fees?
